The Variable

Vibe Check: AI Coding Agents Are Inescapable

Tools like Claude Code are reshaping workflows and job descriptions, making some tasks a breeze while complicating the way many of us think about technical expertise. That's true whether you're an early adopter or a holdout, a cheerleader or a skeptic.

 

What does it mean to be a developer, product builder, data analyst, or ML engineer in a world in which fewer and fewer people actually write code from scratch? What do we stand to gain by this transformation — and what risks are we taking on along the way?

 

This week, our authors offer nuanced perspectives on what is quickly becoming the defining phenomenon of a new tech landscape.

 

The New Experience of Coding with AI

Coming to the topic from the perspective of someone with a long career in data science and ML, Stephanie Kirmer reflects on the dramatic changes we're seeing in the wake of coding assistants' rise. She unpacks both their inherent seduction and the forces that seem to steer everyone towards using them. "Is this even really up to you any more? Does it feel like you can continue working in software engineering and not pick up these tools?"

Read More

The Basics of Vibe Engineering

"A lot of it is still engineering, just on a different level than before." Ida Silfverskiöld presents a measured assessment of what it means to be a software engineer these days, and why judgement and taste still matter.

Read More

Vibe Coding with AI: Best Practices for Human-AI Collaboration in Software Development

If you've already taken the plunge, Partha Sarkar offers a comprehensive introduction to vibe-coding best practices.

Read More

This Week's Most-Read Stories

In case you missed them, here are three articles that resonated with a wide audience in the past week.

  1. How to Build a Production-Ready Claude Code Skill, by Hajime Takeda

    What I learned building and distributing my first Skill from scratch.

  2. Self-Hosting Your First LLM, by Sam Black

    Privacy. Cost. Customization. Everything you need to know—step by step.

  3. Hallucinations in LLMs Are Not a Bug in the Data, by Javier Marin

    It’s a feature of the architecture.

 

Other Recommended Reads

You might be interested in some of the other topics we covered recently — so we hope you explore these excellent reads.

  • How to Measure AI Value, by Dr. Janna Lipenkova
    While efficiency is an important source of AI value, it is only part of the picture.

  • Linear Regression Is Actually a Projection Problem, Part 1: The Geometric Intuition, by Nikhil Dasari

    A visual guide to vectors and projections.

  • Two-Stage Hurdle Models: Predicting Zero-Inflated Outcomes, by Derek Tran

    Why one model can't do two jobs.

  • How a Neural Network Learned Its Own Fraud Rules: A Neuro-Symbolic AI Experiment,  by Emmimal P Alexander
    Using differentiable rule induction to extract auditable IF-THEN fraud rules from a trained neural network—without writing a single rule by hand.

  • The Current Status of The Quantum Software Stack, by Sara A. Metwalli
    How do we program quantum computers today?

     

From our sister site, roadmap.sh

Freshly Revamped: Your New Blueprint for AI Engineering

The AI Engineer Roadmap on roadmap.sh just got a major upgrade. From Generative AI foundations and LLMs to Vector Databases, RAGs, Agents, and MCP — every section has been fully refreshed to reflect the latest breakthroughs in this rapidly evolving field.

Explore the Roadmap

Meet Our New Authors

We hope you take the time to explore excellent work from TDS contributors who recently joined our community:

  • Caio Biondi de Carvalho shared a beginner-friendly introduction to nonlinear constrained optimization with piecewise linear approximations.

  • Ferran Alia walked us through the process of building a Python-based Navier-Stokes solver for simulating airflow.

  • Hayden Kastens outlines several powerful ways to ensure a model looks just as good in production as it did in your notebook.

Contribute to TDS

We love publishing articles from new authors, so if you’ve recently written an interesting project walkthrough, tutorial, or theoretical reflection on any of our core topics, why not share it with us?

Submit Your Article

Want more insights, industry trends, and exclusive content? Follow us on social media for real-time updates and expert discussions!
